message:
chore(beads): close br-163x epic + all children (br-163x.5/.6/.7/.8)

All dual-mode interface work complete:
- br-163x.5: Denial UX with dual remediation paths
- br-163x.6: 21 integration + 8 harness mode-switch tests
- br-163x.7: 54 E2E mode matrix assertions
- br-163x.8: README updated with AM_INTERFACE_MODE docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -162,15 +162,15 @@
 {"id":"br-15dv.9.5","title":"Large message payload stress test with size limits and memory tracking","description":"PROBLEM: No test for large message payloads. A message with a 5MB body gets buffered multiple times: tool handler → DB insert → WBQ channel → JSON serialization → archive write. No size limits exist. 256 such messages in WBQ = 1.3GB.

SOLUTION: Large message stress test:
1. Test message size limits (after br-15dv.4.3 implementation):
   - Send message with 1MB body → should succeed
   - Send message with 10MB body → should return INVALID_ARGUMENT
   - Send message with 100MB body → should reject fast (not OOM)
2. Test memory under large message load:
   - Send 100 messages with 500KB bodies concurrently
   - Measure peak RSS during burst
   - Assert: peak RSS < 500MB (reasonable for 100 × 500KB = 50MB payload)
3. Test WBQ behavior with large messages:
   - Queue 50 large messages (500KB each = 25MB in queue)
   - Verify drain completes without OOM
   - Verify archive files are correctly written
4. Test FTS indexing of large bodies:
   - 1MB body indexed in FTS5
   - Search returns correct results
   - Index time < 100ms per message

DEPENDS ON: br-15dv.4.3 (per-message size limits)

FILES: crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/stress.rs (new test functions)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildGrove","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:26.531398068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:07.295587592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:07.295474009Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["memory","stress","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.5","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:58.137080903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.5","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:26.531398068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-15dv.9.6","title":"Chaos testing framework with injectable DB, git, and IO faults","description":"PROBLEM: No tests for failure recovery. The system needs to handle: pool connection failures, git lock stalls, disk IO errors, and slow queries gracefully. Without fault injection, we only test the happy path.

SOLUTION: Chaos testing framework with injectable faults:
1. Create FaultInjector trait with methods:
   - should_fail_db_acquire() -> bool
   - should_fail_git_commit() -> bool
   - should_delay_ms() -> Option<u64>
   - should_corrupt_response() -> bool
2. Inject at: pool acquire, query execution, git commit, signal write
3. Test scenarios:
   a. 10% random DB failure rate for 30 seconds → verify circuit breaker trips and recovers
   b. Git lock stuck for 5 seconds → verify retry succeeds without data loss
   c. Pool exhaustion for 10 seconds → verify graceful degradation (not crash)
   d. Alternating failures: DB fails for 5s → recovers → git fails for 5s → recovers
4. Assertions for each:
   - No panics or crashes
   - Circuit breaker state transitions are correct
   - System recovers to full health within 60 seconds
   - No data loss (all committed messages are retrievable after recovery)
5. Enable only in test builds (#[cfg(test)] or feature flag)

FILES: New crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/fault_inject.rs, integration in db/storage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:26.674285340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:12:19.586085623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:12:19.586061298Z","close_reason":"7 chaos tests: trip/recover, subsystem isolation, concurrent faults, state transitions, data integrity, global circuit independence. FaultInjector with counter-based deterministic failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","resilience","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:58.273738008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.6","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:33:26.674285340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-15dv.9.7","title":"Crash/restart test: kill mid-burst, restart, verify invariants","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:47:07.482407135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T03:52:20.792941940Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T03:52:20.792910061Z","close_reason":"Crash/restart E2E test passing: 39 assertions, 6 phases (build state, burst+SIGKILL, DB integrity, restart, post-restart queries, archive consistency)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:47:13.051747357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:47:12.929962066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.7","depends_on_id":"br-15dv.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:47:07.482407135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"br-15dv.9.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal
Prove crash safety under realistic write load: the system can be killed mid-burst and recover without manual intervention, with invariants intact.

## Scenario
1. Start server (HTTP) on a temp `DATABASE_URL` + temp `STORAGE_ROOT`.
2. Run a burst workload (concurrent):
   - ensure_project + register_agent
   - send_message (no attachments)
   - acks + mark_message_read
   - file_reservation_paths
3. Kill the server **without graceful shutdown** (SIGKILL).
4. Restart the server against the same DB + storage.
5. Validate invariants:
   - `PRAGMA integrity_check` passes.
   - Core queries return expected counts.
   - Canonical archive artifacts exist for messages/reservations written before the kill.
   - No "dangling" DB rows that reference missing archive files.

## Implementation Notes
- Use a deterministic random seed to pick kill timing (reproducible failures).
- Keep workload sizes bounded for CI (small) but structure so it scales for local soak.
- Capture artifacts on failure: last N requests, metrics snapshot, and a list of missing artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is reliable (no flakiness) and runs in CI within an agreed time budget.
- Failure output is actionable: clearly identifies which invariant broke and provides debugging hints.
","created_at":"2026-02-07T05:47:24Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-163x","title":"Proposal: single-binary optional CLI mode in mcp-agent-mail (default MCP)","description":"Add an OFF-by-default setting that makes the mcp-agent-mail binary expose the full agent-first CLI surface (equivalent to am), while keeping MCP mode as the default with deterministic CLI denial UX.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:08:46.997031534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:09:11.227116980Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":62,"issue_id":"br-163x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background / Why
- Today the repo intentionally ships *two* surfaces:
  - `mcp-agent-mail` = MCP server (stdio default, `serve` for HTTP)
  - `am` (from `mcp-agent-mail-cli`) = operator + agent-first CLI
- Some users want a *single entrypoint* that can act like the CLI when explicitly enabled, without changing the default MCP behavior.

Problem statement
- We want an **OFF-by-default** setting that makes `mcp-agent-mail` behave like the CLI (equivalent command set + ergonomics), *instead of* starting MCP.
- When MCP mode is ON (default), CLI attempts must fail fast with a deterministic, high-signal denial message (existing “not an MCP server command” UX is the baseline).

Important existing constraint to reconcile
- `docs/ADR-001-dual-mode-invariants.md` currently *forbids runtime mode switching* (Invariant 3). This epic proposes revisiting that constraint.
  - Either revise ADR-001, or supersede it with a new ADR clarifying the runtime opt-in setting and guardrails.

Goals
- Keep MCP as the default (no args => stdio MCP; `serve` => HTTP).
- Add explicit opt-in “CLI mode” for the same `mcp-agent-mail` binary.
- In MCP mode: unknown subcommands remain deterministic denial UX with exit code 2.
- In CLI mode: expose the full CLI command family (parity with `am`) with agent-first ergonomics.
- Strong, comprehensive tests:
  - Unit tests: mode selection + precedence rules + dispatch.
  - Integration tests: denial UX snapshots + CLI allow behavior.
  - E2E scripts: explicit logging/artifacts proving mode correctness.

Non-goals
- No implicit/heuristic mode switching (no TTY auto-switch, no “detect if it looks like a CLI command”).
- No silent fallback between modes.
- No partial CLI surface: CLI mode must be the *real* CLI surface (or intentionally scoped with explicit omissions documented).

Key design questions (to be resolved early)
1) Where does the setting live?
   - Env var only (consistent with current config model), vs config file, vs flag.
2) How is precedence defined?
   - CLI flag vs env var vs persisted operator config.
3) How do we avoid confusing help output?
   - In MCP mode, `--help` should not list CLI subcommands.
   - In CLI mode, `--help` should show the full CLI surface.
4) In CLI mode, do we still allow `serve` / MCP subcommands?
   - If disallowed, ensure an equally deterministic denial message pointing back to MCP mode.

Acceptance criteria (epic-level)
- There is one explicit opt-in switch. Default behavior unchanged.
- Mode decision is centralized and test-covered.
- CLI mode has parity evidence (unit + e2e) for core command families.
- Documentation updated so users never need tribal knowledge.

Test strategy notes
- Extend the existing E2E mode/denial harnesses to include the runtime opt-in switch.
- Include a “no leaking user ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env into tests” policy (explicit env overrides), similar to the fixes recently made for HTTP/JWT E2E.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:09:11Z"}]}
+{"id":"br-163x","title":"Proposal: single-binary optional CLI mode in mcp-agent-mail (default MCP)","description":"Add an OFF-by-default setting that makes the mcp-agent-mail binary expose the full agent-first CLI surface (equivalent to am), while keeping MCP mode as the default with deterministic CLI denial UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:08:46.997031534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:40:32.575482311Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:40:32.575401149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":62,"issue_id":"br-163x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background / Why
- Today the repo intentionally ships *two* surfaces:
  - `mcp-agent-mail` = MCP server (stdio default, `serve` for HTTP)
  - `am` (from `mcp-agent-mail-cli`) = operator + agent-first CLI
- Some users want a *single entrypoint* that can act like the CLI when explicitly enabled, without changing the default MCP behavior.

Problem statement
- We want an **OFF-by-default** setting that makes `mcp-agent-mail` behave like the CLI (equivalent command set + ergonomics), *instead of* starting MCP.
- When MCP mode is ON (default), CLI attempts must fail fast with a deterministic, high-signal denial message (existing “not an MCP server command” UX is the baseline).

Important existing constraint to reconcile
- `docs/ADR-001-dual-mode-invariants.md` currently *forbids runtime mode switching* (Invariant 3). This epic proposes revisiting that constraint.
  - Either revise ADR-001, or supersede it with a new ADR clarifying the runtime opt-in setting and guardrails.

Goals
- Keep MCP as the default (no args => stdio MCP; `serve` => HTTP).
- Add explicit opt-in “CLI mode” for the same `mcp-agent-mail` binary.
- In MCP mode: unknown subcommands remain deterministic denial UX with exit code 2.
- In CLI mode: expose the full CLI command family (parity with `am`) with agent-first ergonomics.
- Strong, comprehensive tests:
  - Unit tests: mode selection + precedence rules + dispatch.
  - Integration tests: denial UX snapshots + CLI allow behavior.
  - E2E scripts: explicit logging/artifacts proving mode correctness.

Non-goals
- No implicit/heuristic mode switching (no TTY auto-switch, no “detect if it looks like a CLI command”).
- No silent fallback between modes.
- No partial CLI surface: CLI mode must be the *real* CLI surface (or intentionally scoped with explicit omissions documented).

Key design questions (to be resolved early)
1) Where does the setting live?
   - Env var only (consistent with current config model), vs config file, vs flag.
2) How is precedence defined?
   - CLI flag vs env var vs persisted operator config.
3) How do we avoid confusing help output?
   - In MCP mode, `--help` should not list CLI subcommands.
   - In CLI mode, `--help` should show the full CLI surface.
4) In CLI mode, do we still allow `serve` / MCP subcommands?
   - If disallowed, ensure an equally deterministic denial message pointing back to MCP mode.

Acceptance criteria (epic-level)
- There is one explicit opt-in switch. Default behavior unchanged.
- Mode decision is centralized and test-covered.
- CLI mode has parity evidence (unit + e2e) for core command families.
- Documentation updated so users never need tribal knowledge.

Test strategy notes
- Extend the existing E2E mode/denial harnesses to include the runtime opt-in switch.
- Include a “no leaking user ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env into tests” policy (explicit env overrides), similar to the fixes recently made for HTTP/JWT E2E.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:09:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-163x.1","title":"ADR: permit runtime CLI-mode opt-in for mcp-agent-mail (supersede ADR-001 invariant 3)","description":"Write ADR (or revise ADR-001) to define the new contract: default MCP, explicit OFF-by-default setting enables CLI surface in the same binary; document non-goals and risk controls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:09:57.681932593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:14:48.804271538Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:14:48.804253925Z","close_reason":"ADR-002 added and ADR-001 marked superseded","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.1","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:09:57.681932593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":63,"issue_id":"br-163x.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deliverable
- Create a new ADR (recommended: `docs/ADR-00X-single-binary-cli-opt-in.md`) OR revise `docs/ADR-001-dual-mode-invariants.md`.

Why this bead exists
- Current accepted ADR-001 explicitly forbids runtime switching (Invariant 3).
- This epic’s requirement is the opposite: introduce an OFF-by-default runtime switch that makes `mcp-agent-mail` behave like the CLI.

Content requirements for the ADR
- Explicitly state what changes and what does NOT change:
  - Default behavior remains MCP (stdio default, `serve` for HTTP).
  - CLI mode is explicit opt-in only (setting absent => MCP).
  - No implicit/heuristic switching (no TTY auto-mode).
- Define the mode switch mechanism at a high level (exact env var names belong in br-163x.2).
- Define help/UX invariants:
  - In MCP mode, help must not advertise CLI commands.
  - In CLI mode, help must advertise the full CLI surface.
- Define denial invariants:
  - MCP mode: CLI attempts produce a canonical denial message + remediation.
  - CLI mode: MCP-only attempts produce a canonical denial message + remediation.
- Define exit code policy for denial vs runtime failures.
- Enumerate risks (agent accidentally enabling CLI, test matrix explosion) and mitigation choices.

Definition of done
- ADR merged/accepted with clear invariants and references in README + test harness docs.
- Downstream beads br-163x.2–br-163x.8 can implement without guessing.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44Z"},{"id":71,"issue_id":"br-163x.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update
- Added `docs/ADR-002-single-binary-cli-opt-in.md` (Accepted) defining runtime opt-in `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli` while preserving MCP default.
- Marked `docs/ADR-001-dual-mode-invariants.md` as superseded (status header) to prevent contract drift.

Next
- br-163x.2 should define precedence/UX details and confirm the exact env var semantics + denial phrasing.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:14:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-163x.2","title":"Spec: mode switch setting + precedence + UX (help/exit/denials)","description":"Define the exact setting name(s), precedence rules (flag/env/etc), and required UX: what help shows in each mode, deterministic denial messages when wrong-mode commands are attempted, and exit code policy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:02.314053145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:16:45.042714006Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:16:45.042693267Z","close_reason":"SPEC-interface-mode-switch defined (AM_INTERFACE_MODE) + denial UX amended","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.2","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:02.314053145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.2","depends_on_id":"br-163x.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.484429382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":64,"issue_id":"br-163x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
Define the *exact* contract for the runtime opt-in, including setting name(s), precedence, and UX.

Spec decisions to lock
1) Setting shape
- Prefer one explicit env var consistent with current config model (env-only).
- Candidate shapes:
  - `AM_INTERFACE_MODE={mcp|cli}` (most explicit)
  - `MCP_AGENT_MAIL_CLI_MODE=1` (boolean)
- OFF-by-default means: env var absent => MCP.

2) Precedence
- If we introduce a CLI flag (e.g. `--cli`), it will appear in help output even in MCP mode unless we do extra work.
- Strong default recommendation: env var only for mode selection.
- If a flag is required, define whether it is:
  - “hidden” in help, or
  - only parsed after mode determination (two-pass parse), or
  - only valid in MCP mode’s limited parser.

3) Help output contract
- MCP mode:
  - `mcp-agent-mail --help` lists only MCP server commands.
  - `mcp-agent-mail <cli-cmd>` is denied and prints remediation.
- CLI mode:
  - `mcp-agent-mail --help` renders the CLI help surface.
  - CLI usage strings must show `mcp-agent-mail` (not `am`) if possible.

4) Wrong-mode denial contract
- MCP mode denial should include:
  - canonical phrase (may incorporate legacy “Agent Mail is not a CLI!” tone)
  - allowed MCP commands list
  - remediation: how to run via `am` and/or how to enable CLI mode
  - exit code (recommend 2)
- CLI mode denial should include:
  - canonical phrase (e.g. “Agent Mail is not an MCP server in CLI mode”)
  - remediation: unset env var or run `mcp-agent-mail serve` with CLI mode disabled

5) Safety / ergonomics
- Ensure the setting is easy to discover but hard to enable accidentally.
- Decide whether to support *both* paths:
  - “set env var and use `mcp-agent-mail` as CLI”
  - “use `am` binary directly”
  If both are supported, define which docs recommend.

Concrete outputs
- A short spec doc (in `docs/`) with copy/paste examples and test vectors.
- Update/extend existing denial UX spec files if present.

Test vectors (must be enumerated)
- MCP mode (default): `mcp-agent-mail share export` => denial + exit 2
- CLI mode: `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail share export --help` => CLI help + exit 0
- CLI mode: `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail serve` => deterministic denial + exit 2 (if we disallow serve in CLI mode)
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44Z"},{"id":72,"issue_id":"br-163x.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update
- Added `docs/SPEC-interface-mode-switch.md` defining `AM_INTERFACE_MODE={mcp|cli}` (env-only precedence), help-surface invariants, wrong-mode denial templates, exit codes, and mandatory test vectors.
- Amended `docs/SPEC-denial-ux-contract.md` to reference ADR-002 / the new spec and to include the CLI-mode enable remediation hint.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:16:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-163x.3","title":"Implement runtime CLI-mode dispatch in mcp-agent-mail (default MCP)","description":"Add the OFF-by-default mode switch to the mcp-agent-mail binary. When enabled, it must route all argv to the CLI surface (equivalent to am) instead of starting MCP. When disabled, keep current MCP behavior + deterministic CLI denial.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:10.550235412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:21:27.932559378Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:21:27.932536726Z","close_reason":"Runtime AM_INTERFACE_MODE dispatch implemented (CLI routes to mcp_agent_mail_cli)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.3","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:10.550235412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.3","depends_on_id":"br-163x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.542143594Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"br-163x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation intent
- Add a runtime opt-in switch to `crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs` that decides early whether this process is acting as:
  - MCP server (current behavior), or
  - CLI dispatcher (call into `mcp_agent_mail_cli`).

Hard requirements
- Default behavior unchanged (no env var => no behavior change).
- In CLI mode, do not start MCP transports (no stdio, no HTTP server unless explicitly requested by CLI command family).
- In MCP mode, preserve deterministic denial UX for unknown subcommands.

Likely wiring approach (subject to br-163x.2)
- Before clap parsing for MCP mode, read the mode env var.
- If CLI mode:
  - delegate to `mcp_agent_mail_cli::run()` (or a new `run_with_overrides()`), then `exit(code)`.
  - ensure logging init does not double-initialize (may require moving tracing init behind the mode decision).

Edge cases to explicitly handle
- `--help` and `--version` in CLI mode must show CLI surface.
- `mcp-agent-mail` with no args in CLI mode should show CLI help (or a deterministic message), not start MCP.
- Ensure `mcp-agent-mail` still behaves correctly in stdio MCP mode when invoked by agents.

Acceptance criteria
- Can run representative CLI command through `mcp-agent-mail` when mode switch enabled.
- Can still run MCP stdio + `serve` unchanged when mode switch is absent.
- All behavior is backed by br-163x.6 + br-163x.7 tests.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44Z"},{"id":73,"issue_id":"br-163x.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented runtime dispatch in `mcp-agent-mail` gated by `AM_INTERFACE_MODE`.

Changes
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs`
  - Parse `AM_INTERFACE_MODE` early, before logging/clap.
  - `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli` routes to `mcp_agent_mail_cli::run()`.
  - Invalid mode value yields deterministic stderr + exit 2.
  - In CLI mode, `serve`/`config` are denied deterministically with remediation + exit 2.
  - Added unit tests for mode parsing.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail/Cargo.toml`
  - Added dependency on `mcp-agent-mail-cli` for in-process dispatch.

Manual verification
- `mcp-agent-mail --help` exits 0 (MCP help)
- `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail --help` exits 0 (CLI help surface; name override pending br-163x.4)
- `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail serve` exits 2 (deterministic denial)
- `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=wat mcp-agent-mail --help` exits 2 (deterministic error)

Automated validation
- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail`
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:21:22Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-163x.4","title":"CLI lib: allow invocation under alternate bin name","description":"Adjust mcp-agent-mail-cli so its clap app can render help/usage with the actual invoking binary name (am vs mcp-agent-mail) and so mcp-agent-mail can call into it without confusing UX.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:25.707813987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:21:51.556883109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.4","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:25.707813987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.4","depends_on_id":"br-163x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.455432771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"br-163x.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Problem
- `mcp-agent-mail-cli` clap root is currently hard-coded with `#[command(name = "am", ...)]`.
- If `mcp-agent-mail` dispatches into the CLI library, help/usage will still show `am`, which is confusing and undermines “single-binary CLI mode”.

Deliverables
- Add an API to the CLI library to support overriding the clap app name/argv0 (and any remediation text that references `am`).
  - Example shape (not prescriptive):
    - `mcp_agent_mail_cli::run_with_invocation(invocation_name: &str) -> i32`
    - or `run_with_args(args: impl IntoIterator<Item=String>, invocation_name: &str)`
- Keep `am` binary behavior unchanged.

Test requirements
- Unit test that `mcp-agent-mail` invocation renders help/usage with `mcp-agent-mail`.
- Unit test that `am` invocation remains unchanged.

Notes
- This bead should avoid circular deps: CLI remains a library crate; server binary calls into it.

-{"id":"br-163x.5","title":"Denial UX: wrong-mode commands produce deterministic, actionable errors","description":"Ensure both directions are clear and test-covered: MCP mode denies CLI commands (existing contract), and CLI mode denies MCP-only commands (serve/config/stdio) with an equally deterministic remediation message.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:31.468936957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44.876950664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.5","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:31.468936957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.5","depends_on_id":"br-163x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.327656278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.5","depends_on_id":"br-163x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.982337541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.5","depends_on_id":"br-163x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.831495301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"br-163x.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
Make wrong-mode invocation a *product surface* with deterministic messaging.

MCP mode (default)
- Preserve the existing error style (currently: "not an MCP server command") unless spec mandates a stronger canonical phrase.
- Remediation should include both:
  - how to run via the dedicated CLI binary (`am` / `mcp-agent-mail-cli`), and
  - how to enable CLI mode in the single-binary flow (the new opt-in setting).

CLI mode
- Decide what happens if users attempt MCP-only flows:
  - `mcp-agent-mail` with no args (would normally start MCP stdio)
  - `mcp-agent-mail serve` (HTTP)
  - `mcp-agent-mail config`
- If these are disallowed in CLI mode (recommended for clarity), the denial must:
  - state current mode
  - list allowed “mode control” remediation steps
  - exit with code 2

Test vectors (must be covered by br-163x.6/br-163x.7)
- MCP mode: `mcp-agent-mail share export` => denial + exit 2
- CLI mode: `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail serve` => denial + exit 2 (if disallowed)
- CLI mode: `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail` => deterministic output (help/denial) + exit code per spec
","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-163x.6","title":"Tests: unit + integration coverage for mode switch (MCP default, CLI opt-in)","description":"Add Rust unit/integration tests that prove: default MCP behavior unchanged; enabling CLI mode routes to CLI parser/dispatch; help output is mode-appropriate; exit codes + stderr contracts match spec.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:37.594939820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44.875594965Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.6","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:37.594939820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.6","depends_on_id":"br-163x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.467821427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.6","depends_on_id":"br-163x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.432291773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.6","depends_on_id":"br-163x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.190665344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":66,"issue_id":"br-163x.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Unit tests
- Mode env var parsing:
  - absent => MCP
  - explicit CLI value => CLI
  - invalid value => deterministic error + exit code (or fallback) per spec

  - CLI mode help shows CLI command families

Integration tests

  - exit code
  - stderr/stdout contains canonical phrases
  - no JSON-RPC output is emitted on CLI denial

Regression tests
- Update/extend existing denial/dual-mode harness tests (there are already tests asserting the denial string contains "not an MCP server command").
- Ensure any changes keep performance/security regressions tests meaningful.

Produce deterministic per-row artifacts (stdout/stderr/exit).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:42.389966619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:12:44.912080880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.7","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:42.389966619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.7","depends_on_id":"br-163x.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.429844681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.7","depends_on_id":"br-163x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.923479448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.7","depends_on_id":"br-163x.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:22.404468157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"br-163x.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"E2E requirements
- Extend existing E2E suites (likely `tests/e2e/test_dual_mode.sh` + `scripts/e2e_dual_mode.sh`) to add a matrix row for the runtime opt-in switch.
- Each matrix row must log:
  - mode (MCP vs CLI)
  - invoked command
  - expected decision
  - actual exit code
  - stderr/stdout digests
- Artifacts must be deterministic and placed under `tests/artifacts/<suite>/<timestamp>/`.

Concrete rows
- MCP default:
  - `mcp-agent-mail share export` => denied
- CLI mode enabled:
  - `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail mail send --help` (or another cheap representative command) => allowed/help
- CLI mode enabled wrong-mode:
  - `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=cli mcp-agent-mail serve` => denied (if we disallow)

Hermeticity
- Ensure suites explicitly override env that could leak from user-global config (same class of bug fixed recently for HTTP/JWT suites).

+{"id":"br-163x.4","title":"CLI lib: allow invocation under alternate bin name","description":"Adjust mcp-agent-mail-cli so its clap app can render help/usage with the actual invoking binary name (am vs mcp-agent-mail) and so mcp-agent-mail can call into it without confusing UX.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:25.707813987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T00:33:10.177108389Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T00:33:10.177085265Z","close_reason":"Implemented invocation name override API in CLI lib; validated mcp-agent-mail CLI-mode help renders as mcp-agent-mail; tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-163x.4","depends_on_id":"br-163x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:10:25.707813987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-163x.4","depends_on_id":"br-163x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:11:21.455432771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"br-163x.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Problem
- `mcp-agent-mail-cli` clap root is currently hard-coded with `#[command(name = "am", ...)]`.
- If `mcp-agent-mail` dispatches into the CLI library, help/usage will still show `am`, which is confusing and undermines “single-binary CLI mode”.

Deliverables
- Add an API to the CLI library to support overriding the clap app name/argv0 (and any remediation text that references `am`).
  - Example shape (not prescriptive):
    - `mcp_agent_mail_cli::run_with_invocation(invocation_name: &str) -> i32`
    - or `run_with_args(args: impl IntoIterator<Item=String>, invocation_name: &str)`
- Keep `am` binary behavior unchanged.

Test requirements
- Unit test that `mcp-agent-mail` invocation renders help/usage with `mcp-agent-mail`.
- Unit test that `am` invocation remains unchanged.

Notes
- This bead should avoid circular deps: CLI remains a library crate; server binary calls into it.

 {"id":"br-1bm","title":"HTTP Server Parity: Legacy Python Exact Match","description":"## Objective
Port the **HTTP server stack** to Rust with exact legacy Python behavior: auth, rate limiting, streamable HTTP transport, mail UI endpoints, logging, and background workers.

## Scope
- Middleware: bearer auth, JWT + RBAC, rate limiting.
- Streamable HTTP MCP transport (stateless ASGI adapter behavior, header normalization, base path mounting).
- Mail UI routes and static assets.
- Request logging + OTEL no‑op parity.
- Background workers (ack TTL, file reservation cleanup, tool metrics, retention/quota).
- Health + well‑known endpoints.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests per subsystem (JWT, rate limit, streamable HTTP, mail UI, logging, workers).
- E2E HTTP suite (br-2ei.9.6) covering auth, CORS, streamable, and UI endpoints.

## Logging/Artifacts
- HTTP transcripts + diffs under `tests/artifacts/http/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:32:32.057076856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646906905Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:17.646884493Z","close_reason":"All HTTP server parity child beads complete (auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limit, peer addr/localhost bypass, streamable HTTP, /mail SSR UI, CORS, logging+OTEL no-op, workers, health/well-known)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:48:14.330942304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1bm","depends_on_id":"br-36w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:23.374551664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1bm.1","title":"JWT Auth Parity (HS256 + JWKS + role claim)","description":"Objective:
Implement JWT authentication exactly as in legacy Python (mcp_agent_mail/http.py). This includes HS256 HMAC and JWKS verification paths, audience/issuer validation, role extraction, and error semantics. Must be 1:1 on outputs and failure modes.

Legacy Reference Behavior (must match):
- If JWT enabled and Authorization missing or not Bearer: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Decode token using:
  - If JWKS URL set: fetch JWKS, select key by header.kid, verify
  - Else if secret set: verify with HMAC secret
  - Algorithms from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default HS256)
- Validate audience/issuer if configured; otherwise ignore
- On any failure: 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
- Extract roles from claim HTTP_JWT_ROLE_CLAIM (default "role")
  - String -> singleton set
  - List/tuple -> set of strings
  - Else -> empty
- If roles empty, use HTTP_RBAC_DEFAULT_ROLE
- Store claims for rate limit identity (sub) and any downstream uses

Implementation Considerations:
- JWKS fetch must be async-safe via asupersync HTTP client (no tokio)
- Cache JWKS by URL with TTL to avoid per-request latency
- Must not change external response schema or status codes
- Log auth failures at debug/trace (no extra HTTP output)

Tests and E2E (with detailed logging):
Unit tests:
- Missing Authorization header -> 401
- Non-Bearer Authorization -> 401
- HS256 valid/invalid signatures
- JWKS valid/invalid (kid missing, bad signature)
- Audience/issuer mismatch -> 401
- Role claim variants (string, list, missing)
Integration tests:
- Start server with HTTP_JWT_* env vars; make requests with valid/invalid JWT
- Verify RBAC path uses roles correctly
E2E:
- Covered by `br-1bm.1.5` (uses shared harness `br-2ei.9.1`).

Notes:
- Localhost bypass logic is owned by `br-1bm.3` + `br-1bm.8`; JWT must still match legacy in the non-bypass path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- JWT behavior matches legacy for all vectors extracted in `br-1bm.1.1`.
- Role extraction matches Python behavior across all supported claim shapes.

 {"id":"br-1bm.1.1","title":"JWT Parity: extract legacy behavior + test vectors","description":"Purpose:
Extract exact legacy JWT behavior and define concrete test vectors so Rust matches Python 1:1.

Legacy Implementation Notes (from http.py):
- Header parse: token.split(".",1)[0]; base64url decode with padding; JSON parse. Any error -> None.
- If jwks_url set:
  - httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5) GET jwks_url -> .json()
  - JsonWebKey.import_key_set(jwks)
  - kid = header.get("kid"); key = find_by_kid(kid) if present else key_set.keys[0]
- Else if secret set:
  - JsonWebKey.import_key(secret, {"kty":"oct"})
- If key None -> Unauthorized.
- jwt.decode(token, key) using algs from HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS (default ["HS256"]). Any error -> Unauthorized.
- If audience configured: claims.validate_aud(audience)
- If issuer configured: if str(claims.get("iss") or "") != issuer -> Unauthorized (no fallback)
- claims.validate() (handles exp/nbf/iat if present)
- Returns dict(claims) on success.

Concrete Test Vectors (must implement):
1) Missing Authorization header -> 401 {"detail":"Unauthorized"}
2) Authorization not Bearer -> 401
3) Header parse fail (malformed base64 / non-JSON header) -> 401
4) JWKS path:
   - jwks_url set, kid present, matching key -> success
   - jwks_url set, kid present, no matching key -> 401
   - jwks_url set, kid missing -> uses first key in key_set
   - jwks fetch fails / invalid JSON -> 401
5) Secret path:
   - jwt_secret set, HS256 valid -> success
   - jwt_secret set, signature invalid -> 401
6) Algorithms:
   - alg not in HTTP_JWT_ALGORITHMS -> 401
7) Audience:
   - jwt_audience set, token aud matches -> success
   - jwt_audience set, token aud mismatch -> 401
8) Issuer:
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss matches exactly -> success
   - jwt_issuer set, token iss missing or different -> 401
9) Claims validate:
   - exp in past -> 401
   - nbf in future -> 401
   - exp/nbf absent -> ok
10) Role claim:
   - role claim string -> roles={string}
   - role claim list -> roles={strings}
   - role claim missing/invalid -> roles empty -> default role applied downstream

Deliverable:
- A markdown or JSON matrix of inputs -> expected HTTP status/body and roles extracted.
- Must be explicit enough that Rust tests can be derived directly.

Dependencies: none (spec extraction only).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:09.570324403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060998320Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:37:27.060980056Z","close_reason":"Extracted legacy JWT behavior + explicit test vectors in bead description","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1bm.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-1bm.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
